name: Urbie Green
abstract: Urban Clifford "Urbie" Green (August 8, 1926 – December 31, 2018) was an American jazz trombonist who toured with Woody Herman, Gene Krupa, Jan Savitt, and Frankie Carle. He played on over 250 recordings and released more than twenty albums as a soloist.

interviews.title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

quotes.text: Like the Carnegie Hall jazz thing that Benny Goodman did I think with just one microphone, so they’d balance themselves. And over the years it’s gotten so everybody is really isolated in the studios now. You’ve got your own microphone and your own earphones and so the whole balance is left to the engineer, whereas getting a balance within yourselves is not all that important anymore or critical.
quotes.caption: Recalling Benny Goodman's early Carnegie Hall recording setup
quotes.doc_title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: And it was an amazing band. It was Ray Brown and Jeff Hamilton played the first year on drums, and James Moody and Herb Ellis and Urbie Green and Johnny Coles and Sweets Edison and Jerry Dodgion. I mean it was an amazing band. And we went on the road for three months.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: I had Louie Bellson on drums, Percy Heath and who else, Urbie Green and Bob Dorough and who else did we have there
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: on the octet was Warren Vache, Urbie Green, Bucky Pizzarelli and Hank Jones, Slam Stewart and Benny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Fedchock
about_quotes.evidence: So he was one of my early influences in high school. My first one was Urbie Green
about_quotes.doc_title: John Fedchock interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 28, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: And Urbie I’d met from playing with Benny Goodman and with Red Norvo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Carl Fontana on one of those Dick Gibson Colorado jazz parties, next to Kai Winding, Trummy Young and Urbie Green playing “Undecided”... And I loved them all but then Carl comes in and he’s so smooth and I was like ooh, I like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017
